events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need produce JSON answer. Need analyze scenario: residential HVAC-R furnace & venting, observed PVC flue slope back-pitched (condensing). Work in-progress. Need identify hazards severity immediate controls. Need apply IRC M1801, NFPA 54. Need decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Back-pitched condensing flue can cause flue gas spillage, CO, corrosion, blockage, furnace failure. Severity high maybe critical due to CO poisoning potential. Need component_conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 60? value not relevant. sound_source etc default empty? Schema says string for sound_source, numbers. We can set sound_source: "", event_time:0, rate:0. order empty list, workable empty list. findings: PVC flue is back-pitched causing condensate to pool/block vent; violates minimum slope toward furnace/drain per M1801 and NFPA 54; risk of CO spillage and corrosion. actions: immediate shut down furnace if operating; correct slope to minimum 1/4 inch per foot back toward furnace/inducer drain; support pipe properly; inspect/clear condensate drain; retest combustion/venting; verify manufacturer instructions. references: IRC M1801.3 (vent connectors slope), NFPA 54 12.8? Need be accurate. IRC M1801.3 maybe "Vent connectors shall be installed with a minimum slope of 1/4 unit vertical in 12 units horizontal (2 percent) back toward the appliance." NFPA 54 12.8.4? Let's recall. NFPA 54 National Fuel Gas Code: vent connectors for Category I appliances must slope upward at least 1/4 in/ft toward chimney. For condensing appliances with positive vent pressure, manufacturer instructions require slope back to drain. NFPA 54 12.8.4 maybe "Vent connectors serving appliances equipped with draft hoods or Category I draft hood-equipped appliances shall slope upward not less than 1/4 in/ft from appliance to chimney or vent." For high-efficiency condensing furnaces with PVC venting, NFPA 54 Chapter 12 and manufacturer installation instructions. IRC M1801.3: "Vent connectors serving appliances vented by natural draft shall not be installed up and down more than once and shall slope not less than 1/4 inch per foot and not more than 45 degrees from the horizontal." Actually M1801.3 says connectors shall be installed with minimum slope of 1/4 unit vertical in 12 units horizontal back toward appliance? Let's verify. IRC M1801.3: "Vent connectors shall be installed with a minimum slope of 1/4 unit vertical in 12 units horizontal (2 percent) back toward the appliance, except that horizontal common vents and multiple appliance vents shall be sloped not less than 1/4 unit vertical in 12 units horizontal (2 percent) toward the appliance." Hmm maybe. For condensing furnaces, slope toward drain. Back-pitched means slope away from appliance, causing condensate to collect away from drain. So non-compliant. Need mention immediate controls: stop operation, tag out, correct slope, verify drain, test. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Since actively in-progress and not yet in service, but if furnace operated, critical. The prompt says work in-progress. Hazard severity high. Let's choose high. Could be critical due to CO. The schema includes critical. A competent inspector would likely call high/critical. Since dangerous or non-compliant decision fail. Risk maybe critical because back-pitched condensing vent can cause CO leakage and death. But work in-progress maybe not yet energized. However immediate control required. Let's set risk: critical. But is it critical? Back-pitched PVC flue is a common defect; if furnace runs, condensate blocks vent, pressure switch may trip, but if not, CO can enter home. Potential for CO poisoning is critical. So risk critical. percent_complete maybe 70. value 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0. rate 0. order [] workable [].

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside JSON. We must output exactly one valid JSON object. Need include references. Let's craft.

Need maybe include component_…
